samples.gvd:
module pulse_detect #(
    parameter s0 = 2'b00,
    parameter s1 = 2'b01,
    parameter s2 = 2'b10,
    parameter s3 = 2'b11
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output reg data_out
);

localparam PULSE_DURATION = 3;

reg [1:0] state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        state <= s0;
        data_out <= 1'b0;
    end else begin
        case (state)
            s0: begin
                if (data_in) begin
                    state <= s1;
                end
            end
            s1: begin
                if (data_in) begin
                    state <= s2;
                end
            end
            s2: begin
                if (~data_in) begin
                    state <= s3;
                end
            end
            s3: begin
                if (PULSE_DURATION == 1) begin
                    data_out <= 1'b1;
                    state <= s0;
                end else begin
                    data_out <= 1'b0;
                    state <= s0;
                end
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse Detection Module</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="INITIAL">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="STATE_1">State after detecting first rising edge.</state>
        <state id="STATE_2">State after detecting second falling edge.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State indicating the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the next state.</reg>
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = STATE_1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                STATE_1: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = STATE_2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = STATE_1;
                    end
                end
                STATE_2: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end
                end
                default: next_state = INITIAL;
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= INITIAL;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                data_out <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    INITIAL: data_out <= 1'b0;
                    STATE_1: data_out <= 1'b0;
                    STATE_2: data_out <= 1'b0;
                    PULSE_END: data_out <= 1'b1;
                    default: data_out <= 1'b0;
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Sequential logic for pulse detection based on the state machine.</implementation>
</module>
